## CADO-NFS 3.4.0-modern

The cycle after `3.3.0-modern`, on the fork's own minor line. It keeps upstream
[CADO-NFS](https://gitlab.inria.fr/cado-nfs/cado-nfs) **3.0.0**'s NFS mathematics,
numerics, and parameters **unchanged**. It opens on the now **four-times-confirmed**
premise that on this reference hardware (i9-10850K, Comet Lake — AVX2 but no
AVX-512; one RTX 3090, sm_86) single-machine NFS *speed* is essentially tapped out.
A fresh June-2026 internet survey (upstream CADO; the sethtroisi/MichaelBell forks;
msieve, YAFU, GGNFS; GMP-ECM / ecmongpu / CGBN; pstach/gls; the RSA-250 / DLP-240 /
F_p^4–F_p^6 record papers) reconfirms it: no published technique since ~2010 yields
a >5 % single-machine speedup, and this fork is already ahead of public
implementations on GPU + SIMD modernization. No integer-factoring record has moved
since RSA-250 (Feb 2020).

> **No NFS mathematics is changed, and nothing ships unvalidated.** 3.4.0 keeps the
> v3.3.0 shape — a shippable, *measurable* core plus an honestly-gated research
> track — under the standing gate (`make check` + verified `product == N`, a
> bit-exact check vs GMP / the CPU path, or Intel-SDE validation), and documents
> outcomes even when they are a wash or HW-gated. What is new this cycle is that the
> **headline is itself a measured-on-silicon win**, not a design or a wash.

### Headline — GPU prefactor P-1 / P+1 + adaptive escalating-B1 (C7)

The codebase exploration surfaced one materially new lever: **the GPU pre-NFS
factoring front-end is the only pipeline stage with no Amdahl ceiling** — it is a
*separate stage*, not a fraction of sieve time (which is exactly why it measured
49×/26×/12× in 3.1.0 while in-sieve GPU cofactorization, Amdahl-capped at ~8 % of
sieve, netted <1 %). It ran ECM only. 3.4.0 extends it:

- Pollard **P-1** (finds `p` with `p−1` smooth) and Williams **P+1** (Lucas
  sequences; finds `p` with `p+1` smooth) — stage 1 + stage-2 BSGS — added beside
  the batched ECM (`misc/gpu_prefactor/gpu_pm1_pp1.cuh`), reusing the bit-exact
  multi-precision Montgomery core, interleaved under an **adaptive escalating-B1**
  schedule that stops as soon as the cofactor is prime/1
  (`CADO_PREFACTOR_NOPM1PP1=1` restores ECM-only).
- **14 / 14 bit-exact** vs the host code **and** an independent GMP reference
  (`mpz_powm` for P-1, a GMP Lucas chain for P+1), stage 1 + stage 2, on the RTX
  3090; every recovered factor re-verified `f | N`.
- **~3.3× faster time-to-strip** on a P-1-smooth factor (0.52 s vs 1.73 s ECM-only,
  `product == N` both ways); **~30 ms** overhead when the pre-passes find nothing.
- Honest scope: on a single `N` each P-1/P+1 sequence is **one lane** — its value is
  *coverage* (special-form factors ECM's curve count can miss) and a cheaper
  time-to-strip, **not** GPU throughput, which remains ECM's thousands of parallel
  curves. (`docs/gpu-prefactor-pm1pp1-c7.md`, `BENCHMARKS.md` §8.1.)

### Usability / observability core (E9–E12) — all runs on this box, default-off

- **E9 — notifications.** `cado-nfs.py --notify CHANNELS` sends a completion/failure
  notification through any of **desktop / ntfy / Slack / Discord / webhook / email**
  (`scripts/cadofactor/notify.py`, dependency-free, doctested). Secrets (webhook
  URLs, SMTP credentials) come from the environment or the live `[notifications]`
  block — **never the committed parameter snapshot**. Wired through a `status.py`
  finish hook, so it covers normal, error, **and** GPU-prefactor-only completions; a
  channel error is logged and skipped, never affecting the factorization.
- **E10 — structured event log + Prometheus `/metrics`.** `--json-log FILE` appends
  an NDJSON event stream (`run_start` / `phase_start` / `run_finish`, one JSON object
  per line — 14 lines on a 59-digit smoke run). A `/metrics` endpoint renders the
  live status as Prometheus text exposition on **both** the Flask `api_server.py`
  (`cado_nfs_*`) and the Rust `cado-wu-server` (`cado_wu_*` from the wudb).
- **E11 — multi-run history DB.** `~/.cado-nfs/runs.db` (SQLite, override
  `CADO_RUNS_DB`) records one row per terminal run (`scripts/cadofactor/runs.py`,
  doctested); **`--list-runs`** and **`--compare-runs SPEC`** (recent / by-digits with
  min·mean·max wall / `A:B` side-by-side).
- **E12 — per-phase ETA + wizard + completions.** `cado-nfs-monitor-rs` and the
  `/dashboard` reset their trend window on a phase change so the ETA is scoped to the
  current phase, and add an **all-phases ETA**; **`--wizard`** is an interactive
  parameter TUI (`scripts/cadofactor/wizard.py`, doctested) that prints a ready-to-run
  command line; the argparse-generated completions pick up every new flag (63 option
  strings) with path-aware completion for the file-valued ones, and `misc/man/cado-nfs.1`
  gains a monitoring/history section + worked EXAMPLES. (`docs/usability-v340.md`.)

### Data-driven autotuner (A7)

The static `--plan` wall-time model is anchored on one reference box; once a host
has real runs in `runs.db`, 3.4.0 refines the estimate from the host's **own**
measurements — **without touching any number-theoretic bound** (`lim*`/`lpb*`/`mfb*`/`I`):

- **`--calibrate`** backs out this host's per-core speed factor (the geometric mean
  of the per-run implied factors) and prints the suggested `--host-speed` value plus
  the regression fit quality.
- A **log-linear OLS cost model** (`planner.regression_estimate`) over the host's
  runs is folded into `--plan` *alongside* the static model, clearly labelled with
  its sample count and basis. Seeded validation: 5 runs at a clean 1.5× host speed
  recover **1.500×** and an **R² of 0.978**, with the empirical refinement inside the
  reported ±20 % band. (`docs/usability-v340.md`.)

### Research track — validated kernels, honest non-wins (C5+/C6+)

- **C5+ — GPU root-sieve conditional-launch threshold**
  (`bench/gpu-ropt-threshold-c5plus.cu`). The v3.3.0 C5 kernel was bit-exact but a
  wash at desktop sizes; shipping it unconditionally would regress small runs. C5+
  adds a calibrated launch heuristic that routes each size to the **measured-faster**
  path — **bit-exact at every swept size**, CPU below the crossover, GPU above it
  (~16 M-cell lines / ~2×10⁸ scatter updates, where the GPU pulls **~3.9×** ahead).
  It quantifies C5's "large-N only" finding and unlocks the kernel with no small-N
  regression.
- **C6+ — GPU GF(p) lingen NTT multi-modular CRT wrapper**
  (`bench/gpu-lingen-ntt-crt-c6plus.cu`). The piece C6 said real GF(p) coefficients
  need: reduce mod K NTT primes, GPU-NTT-multiply mod each, CRT-reconstruct. Four
  primes (product ~2¹²³), degree 1500×1500: CRT reconstruction **== `__int128`
  integer convolution, 0 / 2999 wrong**, and 0 / 2999 after reduction mod a ~107-bit
  prime. Validates the *mechanism*, not a single-machine win (lingen is ~3–8 % of
  BWC). Both run via `bench/gpu-research-v340-validate.sh`.

### Compatibility & build

- **Drop-in for 3.3.0-modern.** The default build is unchanged (no NFS-algorithm or
  CPU-path change); `CADO_VERSION_STRING` is now `3.4.0-modern` (`CMakeLists.txt`
  `CADO_VERSION_MINOR 3 → 4`); no test depends on the string. Everything new is
  additive and off by default.
- New Python modules (`notify.py`, `runs.py`, `wizard.py`) are doctested
  (`test_python_{notify,runs,wizard}`); the GPU C7/C5+/C6+ benches need nvcc/CUDA 13
  + an NVIDIA GPU and are standalone (no CADO build). Toolchain floor unchanged:
  C99 + C++20, CMake ≥ 3.18, GMP ≥ 5 `--enable-shared`.

### Validation summary

`product == N` end-to-end on the c59 smoke with `--notify` + `--json-log` (14 event
lines, run recorded in `runs.db`); the new `notify`/`runs`/`wizard` doctests pass via
ctest under the configured `PYTHON_EXECUTABLE` (7/7 with the touched modules); **C7
14/14 bit-exact vs CPU & GMP, ~3.3× time-to-strip**; A7 recovers a seeded 1.500×
(R²=0.978); C5+ bit-exact every size with correct routing; C6+ CRT == `__int128`
convolution (0/2999); `cargo build` + `cargo fmt --check` + `clippy` clean on the
workspace; `ruff` clean on the new/modified Python. See `CHANGELOG.md`,
`BENCHMARKS.md` §8, `docs/ROADMAP-v3.4.0-modern.md`, and the per-track docs for the
full methodology and the honest scope of every item.

---

*This is a modernization + performance fork, **not** the official CADO-NFS. For
academic use, cite the upstream project (see `AUTHORS`). Released under LGPL-2.1,
preserving all upstream copyright and authorship. Prior releases: `v3.3.0-modern`,
`v3.2.0-modern`, `v3.1.0-modern`, `v3.0.0-modern`, `v2.3.1-modern`.*
